biography.value: Hildvarð Bruneson is the DjÃºpdvergr Forgemaster of DjÃºphól â€” a 140-year-old craftsman who has maintained his forge and his people through the Fimbulwinter through sheer stubbornness and skill. He is deeply weathered, blunt, and fair. His forge still burns. He will make a deal with anyone who can help him keep it that way.

description.value:
Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 50/100 ft., one target.

Hit: 0 (0) bludgeoning damage.

When you make this strike, choose the damage type from one of the following options: acid, cold, corruption, fire, lightning, poison, or sonic.

Choose one of the following effects:

  • You use the @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.emug9cXuwndDrWzu]{Knockback} bonus action, but its distance becomes the range of your Hurl Element ability, and you use Reason instead of Might for the ability check.

  • You choose a creature within the distance of your Hurl Element ability and one of the following damage types: . That creature takes damage of the chosen type equal to your Reason score.

  • You teleport up to a number of feet equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}. If you choose this option, you can spend essence to teleport 1 additional feet (5 ft.) for each essence spent.

You use your immersion in the mystery of void magic to expand the reach of that magic better than other mages. The distance of all your abilities that have the Magic, Ranged, and Void keywords increases by 2 feet.

You instantly recognize illusions for what they are, you can see invisible creatures, and supernatural effects canâ€™t conceal creatures and objects from you. Additionally, you always know if an area or object you observe is magical or affected by magic, and you know the specifics of what that magic can do.

You also gain the @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.LgMzJ9xHxs6ggP1y]{Shared Void Sense} ability.

For each Victory you have, you can target one creature. Each target gains the benefit of your @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.classes.Z6v8ZacedvuaVoy4]{A Beyonding of Vision} feature until the end of your next turn, but doesnâ€™t gain the use of the Shared Void Sense ability.
You teleport the target up to a number of feet equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}. If the target moves to trigger this ability, you can teleport them at any point during the move.

You open two size 1 portals in unoccupied spaces within distance, which last until you move beyond distance from any portal, end the effect as a bonus action, or are dying. Each portal must be placed at a height of no more than 1 feet (5 ft.) above the ground. When you or any ally touch a portal, that creature can choose to be instantly teleported to an unoccupied space of their choice adjacent to the other portal. If an enemy is force moved into a portal, their forced movement ends and they emerge from the other portal in an unoccupied space chosen by the creature who force moved them.

At the start of each of your turns while the portals are active, you can open a new portal connected to the others. If three or more por tals are present, you and your allies choose which portal to emerge from when entering a portal, and a creature who force moves an enemy into a portal chooses that enemyâ€™s destination portal.

Each time you finish a long rest, you can open a two-way portal that leads to any place you have previously been. You and your allies can pass through the portal, which remains open for 1 hour or until you dismiss it as a main action.

While you have 3 or more essence and are not dying, you exude an aura of magic whose distance is equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}. The effects within the area of the aura are based on your specialization, as shown on the Mantle of Essence Specialization Effects table. You can activate and deactivate the aura at will (no action required).
The area provides concealment for you and your allies.

Solidity is merely a suggestion to you. Mundane barriers that are 1 feet (5 ft.) thick or less do not block your senses or line of effect. You can only sense or have line of effect past one such barrier at a time.

Additionally, whenever you use a void ability, you or one ally within distance of the ability can teleport a number of feet equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}.
A protective field of void magic absorbs violence aimed at you, then lets you hurl it back at your enemies. The first time each round that you take damage, you .

You can wear light armor and wield light weapons effectively, even though you donâ€™t have a kit. While you wear light armor, you gain a +3 bonus to Hit Points, and that bonus increases by 3 at 4th, 7th, and 10th levels. While you wield a light weapon, you gain a +1 damage bonus with weapon abilities, including free strikes. You can use light armor treasures and light weapon treasures.

If you have a kit, you canâ€™t take this enchantment.

You channel the substance of creation in the form of a Heroic Resource called essence, gathering and burning it to cast and maintain spells.

Essence in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain essence equal to [[lookup @hero.milestones]]{your Milestones}. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 2 essence.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you or a creature within 10 feet takes damage that isnâ€™t untyped or holy damage, you {gain 1 essence}.

You lose any remaining essence at the end of the encounter.

Essence Outside of Combat

Though you canâ€™t gain essence outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost essence without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs essence, you canâ€™t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a long rest.

When you use a persistent ability outside of combat (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.classes.qOIxRq9d3diTKJzO]{Persistent Magic}), you can maintain it for a number of rounds equal to your Milestones.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited essence on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of essence equal to your Milestones.

Some of your heroic abilities have a persistent effect entry. For example, the Instantaneous Excavation ability has an effect noted as â€œPersistent 1.â€ Whenever you use a persistent ability, you decide whether you want to maintain it, and start doing so immediately after you first use the ability. If you maintain a persistent ability in combat, you reduce the amount of essence you earn at the start of your turn by an amount equal to the abilityâ€™s persistent value, which enables the abilityâ€™s persistent effect. All your active persistent abilities end at the end of the encounter.

You canâ€™t maintain any abilities that would make you earn a negative amount of essence at the start of your turn. You can stop maintaining an ability at any time (no action required).

If you maintain the same ability on several targets and the effect includes a ability check, you make that roll once and apply the same effect to all targets. A creature canâ€™t be affected by multiple instances of a persistent ability.

If you take damage equal to or greater than [[lookup 5*@R evaluate=true]]{5 times your Reason score} in one turn, you stop maintaining any persistent abilities. For instance, if you have a Reason score of 2 and are maintaining Instantaneous Excavation, taking 10 or more damage in one turn causes you to stop maintaining the ability.
The first time each combat round that you or a creature within 10 feet takes damage that isnâ€™t untyped or holy damage, you gain 2 essence instead of 1.

The target is teleported to another space within distance. Make a ability check that affects each enemy adjacent to the targetâ€™s new space.

Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 50/100 ft., one target.

Hit: 0 (0) bludgeoning damage.

Melee Weapon Attack: +2 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target.

Hit: 0 (0) bludgeoning damage.

Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 50/100 ft., one target.

Hit: 0 (0) bludgeoning damage.

Choose yourself or one ally within distance. That character can end one effect on them that is ended by a saving throw or that ends at the end of their turn.

Each creature in a 10-foot burst must make a DC 11 Constitution saving throw.

You must be touching the ground to use this ability. Additionally, you can choose a feet (5 ft.) of ground in the area that is unoccupied or is occupied by you or any ally. A pillar of earth rises out of the ground in that feet (5 ft.), with a height in feet up to your Reason score. The pillar canâ€™t collide with any creatures or objects, nor can it force creatures raised by it to collide with other creatures or objects.

Until the start of your next turn, the target can move through solid matter, they ignore difficult terrain, and their movement canâ€™t provoke opportunity attacks. If the target ends their turn inside solid matter, they are forced out into the space where they entered it and this effect ends.

Persistent 1: The effect lasts until the start of your next turn.

Special: The number of creatures you target with this ability is determined by your ability check.

Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 50/100 ft., one target.

Hit: 0 (0) bludgeoning damage.

Each target begins to fade from existence (saveÂ ends). On their first turn while fading from existence, a target takes a disadvantage on power rolls. At the end of their first turn, they have a double disadvantage on power rolls. At the end of their second turn, they fade from existence for 1 hour, after which they reappear in their original space or the nearest unoccupied space.

Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 50/100 ft., one target.

Hit: 0 (0) bludgeoning damage.

When the target ends their next turn, or if they drop to 0 Hit Points before then, each enemy adjacent to them takes {fire damage} equal to twice your Reason score. Each affected enemy then gains this same effect
Choose an enemy adjacent to you. The next ability ability check an ally who makes against that creature before the start of your next turn has an advantage.

A creature who uses the Catch Breath bonus action spends a Hit Die and regains {Hit Points} equal to their recovery value.

A creature who is dying canâ€™t use the Catch Breath bonus action, but other creatures can help them spend Hit Dice in other ways.

When a creature takes the Charge main action, they move up to their speed in a straight line without jumping, then make a @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.wU69Y06G9lYFrvp6]{melee Opportunity Attack} against a target when they end their move. If the creature has an ability with the Charge keyword, they can use that ability against the target instead of a Opportunity Attack.

A creature canâ€™t move through difficult terrain or shift when they charge. They can fly or burrow as part of the Charge main action if they have that movement available to them, but they canâ€™t climb or swim while charging unless they can automatically use that movement at full speed.

Melee Weapon Attack: +2 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target.

Hit: 0 (0) bludgeoning damage.

When a creature takes the {Defend main action}, ability rolls made against them have a double disadvantage until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double advantage on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creatureâ€™s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.

A creature who is grabbed by another creature, an object, or an effect (see Grab below) can attempt to escape by using the following ability.

You take a disadvantage on this bonus action if your size is smaller than the size of the creature, object, or effect that has you grabbed.

A creature seeking to keep a foe close and locked down can attempt to grab a creature using the following ability

Melee Weapon Attack: +2 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target.

Hit: 0 (0) bludgeoning damage.

You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

A creature who uses the Heal main action employs medicine or inspiring words to make an adjacent creature feel better and stay in the fight. The target creature can spend a Hit Die to regain Hit Points, or can make a saving throw against one effect they are suffering that is ended by a saving throw.

Melee Weapon Attack: +2 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target.

Hit: 0 (0) bludgeoning damage.

You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

Melee Weapon Attack: +2 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target.

Hit: 0 (0) bludgeoning damage.

Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 25/50 ft., one target.

Hit: 0 (0) bludgeoning damage.
A creature can use the Stand Up bonus action to stand up if they are prone, ending that condition. Alternatively, they can use this bonus action to make a willing adjacent prone creature stand up.
When a creature takes the Advance move action, they move a number of feet up to their speed. They can break up this movement with their bonus action and main action however they wish.

When a creature takes the Disengage move action, they can shift [[lookup @movement.disengage]]{1} feet (5 ft.)(s). Certain class features, kits, and other rules allow a creature to shift more than 1 feet (5 ft.) when they disengage. A creature who does so can break up their shift with their bonus action and main action however they wish.

A creature can take the Ride move action only while mounted on another creature. When a creature takes the Ride move action, they cause their mount to move up to the mountâ€™s speed, taking the rider with them. Alternatively, a creature can use the Ride move action to have their mount use the Disengage move action as a free triggered action. A creature can use the Ride move action only once per round. A mounted creature can only have this move action applied to them once per round. This movement can be broken up with the riderâ€™s bonus action and main action however they wish.
